Title: User Registration Validation
Preconditions: User has opened the registration form in the app.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., test@example.com), valid password (8+ characters), valid name (e.g., "John Doe")
Test Steps:
1. User enters valid email address in the email field.
2. User enters valid password in the password field.
3. User enters valid name in the name field.
4. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result: System validates inputs and creates a new user account with the provided details. User receives a confirmation message.

Title: User Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User has registered and confirmed an account.
Test Steps:
1. User enters registered email in the email field.
2. User enters correct password in the password field.
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result: System authenticates the user and grants access to the app’s main dashboard.

Title: User Login with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: User has opened the login screen.
Test Steps:
1. User enters registered email in the email field.
2. User enters incorrect password in the password field.
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result: System displays an error message: "Invalid email or password."

Title: Display User Carbon Footprint Summary
Preconditions: User is logged in and has recorded at least one activity.
Test Data: User with recorded activities (e.g., travel, energy usage)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Carbon Footprint" section.
2. User selects the "Summary" option.
Expected Result: System displays a summary of the user’s total carbon footprint, broken down by activity type (e.g., travel, energy, food).

Title: Record New Activity for Carbon Footprint
Preconditions: User is logged in and in the "Add Activity" screen.
Test Data: Activity type (e.g., "Travel"), distance (e.g., 100 km), transport method (e.g., "Car")
Test Steps:
1. User selects "Travel" as the activity type.
2. User enters "100" in the distance field.
3. User selects "Car" as the transport method.
4. User clicks the "Save" button.
Expected Result: System calculates and records the carbon footprint for the activity. User sees a confirmation message: "Activity saved successfully."

Title: System Event Trigger for Carbon Footprint Update
Preconditions: User has recorded activities and the system is connected to the server.
Test Data: System event triggered by a scheduled update (e.g., daily carbon footprint recalculation)
Test Steps:
1. System detects a scheduled event for carbon footprint recalculation.
2. System fetches updated data from the server.
Expected Result: System recalculates the user’s carbon footprint and updates the database. User receives a notification: "Your carbon footprint has been updated."

Title: Store User Activity Data in Database
Preconditions: User has recorded an activity and the system is connected to the server.
Test Data: Activity details (e.g., type: "Travel", distance: 100 km, transport: "Car"), user ID
Test Steps:
1. User records an activity (as per TC-CARBONFOOTPRINT-002).
2. System sends activity data to the server.
Expected Result: Database stores the activity data under the user’s record with a unique ID. Data includes activity type, distance, transport method, and timestamp.

Title: Retrieve User Carbon Footprint Data from Database
Preconditions: User is logged in and has recorded activities.
Test Data: User ID with existing carbon footprint data
Test Steps:
1. User requests to view carbon footprint summary.
2. System queries the database for user’s carbon footprint data.
Expected Result: System retrieves and displays the user’s carbon footprint data, including all recorded activities and their emissions.

Title: Server API Response for Carbon Footprint Calculation
Preconditions: User has recorded an activity and the system is connected to the server.
Test Data: Activity details (e.g., type: "Travel", distance: 100 km), API endpoint for carbon calculation
Test Steps:
1. System sends activity data to the server via API.
2. Server processes the request and returns a carbon footprint value.
Expected Result: System receives a valid JSON response with the calculated carbon footprint value (e.g., {"footprint": 20.5}). System updates the user’s record accordingly.

Title: Accessibility for Screen Reader Users
Preconditions: Screen reader software (e.g., TalkBack) is enabled on the device.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Carbon Footprint" section using screen reader.
2. User requests screen reader to read the summary of carbon footprint data.
Expected Result: Screen reader accurately reads all displayed carbon footprint data, including activity types, values, and summary statistics.

Title: Secure User Data Transmission
Preconditions: User is logged in and attempting to record an activity.
Test Data: Activity details (e.g., type: "Travel", distance: 100 km), HTTPS connection enabled
Test Steps:
1. User records an activity.
2. System transmits data to the server over HTTPS.
Expected Result: Data is encrypted during transmission. Server validates the user’s session token before processing the request. No warnings or errors are displayed to the user.

Title: Update Application Without Data Loss
Preconditions: User has recorded activities and the app is installed on the device.
Test Data: Updated app version (e.g., v2.0), user data stored locally and on the server
Test Steps:
1. User installs the updated app version.
2. User logs in to the app.
Expected Result: All previously recorded activities and carbon footprint data are preserved and accessible. No data loss occurs during the update process.

Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: App installed on the device
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app for the first time.
2. Select the option to create a new account.
3. Fill in the registration form with valid username, password, and email.
4. Submit the form.
Expected Result: User is notified of successful registration and redirected to the Home page. User data and device info are saved in the database.

Title: Duplicate Username Registration Attempt
Preconditions: App installed on the device, an existing account with the same username
Test Data: Username already registered, valid password, email
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app for the first time.
2. Select the option to create a new account.
3. Fill in the registration form with an existing username, valid password, and email.
4. Submit the form.
Expected Result: Error notification stating the username is already in use. System suggests trying to log in instead.

Title: Successful User Login
Preconditions: App installed, user has an existing account
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app.
2. Enter valid username and password in the login form.
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result: User is authenticated and redirected to the main application page. Device data is saved in the database.

Title: Failed Login with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: App installed, user has an existing account
Test Steps:
1. Launch the app.
2. Enter incorrect username or password in the login form.
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result: Error notification prompting to re-enter credentials. Option to create a new account is displayed.

Title: View User Profile
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the Profile page.
2. Click the button to view profile.
Expected Result: User’s account details (username, email) are displayed. Links to modify profile or password are visible.

Title: Modify User Profile
Preconditions: User is on the Profile page
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Modify Profile" button.
2. Update email or password fields.
3. Submit changes.
Expected Result: Updated data is saved in the database. User is redirected to the Profile page with updated details.

Title: Discard Profile Modifications
Preconditions: User is on the Profile page, modifications are made
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Modify Profile" button.
2. Update email or password fields.
3. Click the "Cancel" button.
Expected Result: Changes are discarded. User remains on the Profile page with original data.

Title: Delete User Profile
Preconditions: User is on the Profile page
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Delete Account" button.
2. Confirm deletion.
Expected Result: User data is removed from the database. User is redirected to the Login page.

Title: Cancel Profile Deletion
Preconditions: User is on the Profile page
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Delete Account" button.
2. Click the "Cancel" button.
Expected Result: Deletion is canceled. User remains on the Profile page.

Title: Successful Logout
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Profile page
Test Steps: 1. Click the "Logout" button.
Expected Result: User is disconnected and redirected to the Login page.

Title: View Carbon Footprint
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps: 1. Click the "Rilevamento del Footprint" button.
Expected Result: The last calculated Carbon Footprint is displayed on the screen.

Title: Hardware Scan Execution
Preconditions: User is on the Hardware Scan page
Test Steps: 1. Click the "Scansione" button.
Expected Result: System retrieves hardware component data via API and displays it to the user.

Title: Update Device Components
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Cambio componenti del dispositivo" button.
2. Complete the questionnaire confirming component replacement.
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result: Database is updated with the recalculated Carbon Footprint.

Title: Exit Component Update Without Confirmation
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Click the "Cambio componenti del dispositivo" button.
2. Click the "Exit" button without confirming any changes.
Expected Result: User exits the page without updating the database.

Title: Privacy Policy Acceptance on First Access
Preconditions: User has not accessed the application before
Test Steps:
1. User opens the application for the first time
2. System displays privacy policy and usage terms
3. User reads the terms
4. User accepts the terms by clicking "Accept"
Expected Result: User is granted access to the application and can proceed to registration/login

Title: User Profile Registration
Preconditions: User has accepted privacy policy
Test Data: Valid email, password, name, optional fields (e.g., device model)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to registration screen
2. User fills mandatory fields (email, password, name)
3. User optionally fills additional fields (e.g., device model)
4. User clicks "Register" button
Expected Result: User profile is created successfully, and user is redirected to login or dashboard

Title: Update User Profile Information
Preconditions: User is logged in with an existing profile
Test Data: Updated email, name, or optional fields (e.g., device model)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to profile settings
2. User modifies one or more fields (e.g., email, name)
3. User clicks "Save" or "Update" button
Expected Result: Profile updates successfully, and changes are reflected in the system

Title: Log Out from Application
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to logout option
2. User confirms logout action
Expected Result: User session terminates, and user is redirected to login screen or home screen without access to profile data

Title: Display Carbon Footprint Calculation
Preconditions: User is logged in and has registered a device
Test Data: Device model, component type (e.g., battery, screen), or full device replacement
Test Steps:
1. User selects option to calculate carbon footprint
2. User inputs device model and selects component or full replacement
3. User confirms calculation
Expected Result: System displays the calculated carbon footprint impact for the selected action

Title: Data Deletion Upon Uninstallation
Preconditions: User has installed the application and logged in
Test Steps: 1. User uninstalls the application from the device
Expected Result: All local application data is permanently deleted from the device

Title: Elimination of Personal Data Upon Account Deletion
Preconditions: User has an active account with personal data stored
Test Steps: 1. User deletes their account (as per TC-USERPROFILE-004)
Expected Result: All personal data and sensitive information associated with the user are permanently deleted from the database

Title: Hardware Scanning and Data Retrieval
Preconditions: User is logged in and has a compatible device
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to hardware scan option
2. User grants necessary permissions for data access
3. System scans device for memory, battery status, and model information
Expected Result: System displays a summary screen with memory usage, battery health, and device model details

Title: Verify Database Query Response Time
Preconditions: Application is connected to Firebase database with sample data loaded
Test Data: A query retrieving user profile data (e.g., name, ID)
Test Steps:
1. Execute a database query to fetch user profile data.
2. Measure the time taken for the query to return results.
3. Repeat the test 10 times under normal load conditions.
Expected Result: All query responses must complete within 100 milliseconds.

Title: Validate UI Element Spacing and Size
Preconditions: Application UI is rendered on a standard desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (1080x720) resolution
Test Data: Default UI layout with buttons, dropdowns, and icons
Test Steps:
1. Inspect UI elements (buttons, dropdowns, checkboxes) for minimum spacing between interactive components.
2. Verify that no two selectable elements overlap or are placed too close (e.g., <5px apart).
3. Check that icons/visual cues (e.g., "Add User" button) clearly represent their function without ambiguity.
Expected Result: All interactive elements are spaced adequately (>5px) and visual cues are intuitive.

Title: Test SQL Injection Protection
Preconditions: Application is deployed with a live Firebase database containing test user data
Test Data:
Valid SQL query: `SELECT  FROM users WHERE id = '1'`
Malicious input: `SELECT  FROM users WHERE id = '1' OR '1'='1'`
Test Steps:
1. Submit the valid SQL query via the application’s input field (e.g., search bar).
2. Observe that the query executes and returns expected results.
3. Submit the malicious input via the same field.
4. Verify that the application does not execute the raw input as SQL.
Expected Result: Only the valid query returns data; malicious input is rejected or sanitized.

Title: Verify MVC Design Pattern Adherence
Preconditions: Source code repository is accessible with at least one feature module (e.g., user authentication)
Test Steps:
1. Locate the Model component (e.g., `UserModel.java`) and verify it contains only data and business logic (no UI code).
2. Locate the View component (e.g., `UserView.html`) and verify it contains only presentation logic (no business logic).
3. Locate the Controller component (e.g., `UserController.java`) and verify it handles input, updates the Model, and refreshes the View.
Expected Result: Codebase strictly separates Model, View, and Controller responsibilities.

Title: Validate Firebase Database Integration
Preconditions: Application is configured to use Firebase as the primary database
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to write the sample user data to the Firebase database via the application.
2. Verify the data is successfully stored in the Firebase console.
3. Retrieve the data back through the application and confirm it matches the original input.
Expected Result: Data is successfully written to and read from Firebase without errors.

Title: Test Offline Functionality for Critical Features
Preconditions: Application is running on a device with simulated offline mode (no internet)
Test Steps:
1. Disable internet connection on the test device.
2. Attempt to perform a critical action requiring internet (e.g., update user profile).
3. Verify the application displays an appropriate error message (e.g., "No internet connection").
4. Re-enable internet and retry the action.
Expected Result: Action fails gracefully offline with a clear error; succeeds upon reconnection.

Title: Validate Data Usage Compliance
Preconditions: Application is deployed with user data collection enabled
Test Data: Sample user data including name, email, and location (if applicable)
Test Steps:
1. Collect user data via the application (e.g., during registration).
2. Verify the application does not store or transmit data beyond what is necessary for its core functions.
3. Check that user data is encrypted during transmission (if applicable).
4. Confirm the application includes a privacy policy link explaining data usage.
Expected Result: Data is collected, stored, and processed in compliance with GDPR and Italian laws; no unnecessary data is retained.

Title: User Informed About Resource Usage and Privacy
Preconditions: Application is downloaded and launched
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application for the first time.
2. Observe any pop-up or screen informing the user about resource usage and privacy.
Expected Result: User is shown a notification or screen explaining resource usage and privacy policies.

Title: User Account Creation
Preconditions: Application is downloaded from the store
Test Steps:
1. Open the application.
2. Navigate to the registration page.
3. Enter valid user credentials (email, password, name).
4. Submit the registration form.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the login page, and their data is saved in the database.

Title: Modify User Account Data
Preconditions: User has completed registration and logged in
Test Data: Updated user details (e.g., new email, phone number)
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Navigate to the account settings or profile section.
3. Select the option to modify account data.
4. Enter updated user details.
5. Save the changes.
Expected Result: User data is updated in the database.

Title: User Logout
Preconditions: User has logged in to the application
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Navigate to the logout option.
3. Confirm logout.
Expected Result: User is no longer connected to the application.

Title: User Account Deletion
Preconditions: User has an active account
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Navigate to the account settings or profile section.
3. Select the option to delete the account.
4. Confirm the deletion.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the login page, their account data is deleted from the database, and they no longer have an account.

Title: Display Ecological Impact Information
Preconditions: Application is installed
Test Steps:
1. Open the application.
2. Navigate to the section displaying ecological impact information.
Expected Result: User is shown their ecological footprint information on a dedicated page.

Title: Display Device Hardware Information
Preconditions: User has logged in
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Navigate to the device information section.
Expected Result: User sees details about memory, device model, and battery.

Title: Delete User Data
Preconditions: User has logged in
Test Steps:
1. Log in to the application.
2. Navigate to the data deletion option.
3. Confirm the deletion of personal data.
Expected Result: User data is removed from the database, and the user is no longer part of the community.

Title: Application Disinstallation
Preconditions: Application is installed on the device
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the device’s application manager.
2. Locate the application.
3. Select the option to uninstall.
4. Confirm the uninstallation.
Expected Result: Application is no longer present on the device.
